Today Library Congress named Natasha Trethewey 19th U.S. poet laureate, succeeding Philip Levine.
Trethewey joins long line distinguished poets have served position including W.S.
Merwin, Ryan, Charles Simic, Donald Hall, Kooser, Louise Glück, Billy Collins, Stanley Kunitz, Robert Pinsky, Robert Hass Rita Dove.
Natasha Trethewey Pulitzer Prize-winning author three poetry collections, including Native Guard (2006), winner 2007 Pulitzer Prize Poetry, Bellocq's Ophelia (2002), Domestic Work (2000).
newest collection poems, Thrall, scheduled appear this fall.
Trethewey also author nonfiction book, Beyond Katrina: Meditation Mississippi Gulf Coast (2010).
English creative writing professor Emory University Atlanta.
Threthewey born Gulfport, Mississippi, 1966.
also Mississippi's poet will first person serve simultaneously state U.S. laureate.
Here's audio clip from that includes reading poet laureate also click here more information.

started, navigate Automation Variable Library HelloID Administrator Dashboard.

this example, we'll choose string.
Provide Name variable.
Spaces allowed.
separate words, CamelCase Snake_Case.
this example, we'll call variable "exchange_server".
Provide value variable that corresponds with chosen data type.
this example, we'll provide value "exchange01".
Click plus sign button variable your HelloID environment.
biggest benefit using variables that they reduce effort updating value that used multiple places.
Rather than having make update multiple places, simply update variables value.
Wherever that variable used, value will present when variable evaluated.
update variable's value, change value Value column click Save button Variable Library window.

value variable sensitive, should protected from shoulder-surfing, then hide value form clicking icon Actions column.
